summary: Install-time backdoor and credential exfiltration to hardcoded C2
details: cursed-modules@999.0.6 is a hostile npm package that runs install.js via preinstall/install/postinstall lifecycle hooks and on require() of the main module. On install, it reads /root/.ssh/id_rsa, /etc/hosts, the full process.env, hostname, cwd and a recursive directory listing, base64-encodes them, and PUTs the result to a hardcoded C2 at 154.57.164.82:30843. It then enters a polling loop that fetches attacker-supplied commands from the same host, executes them via child_process.execSync, and PUTs the output back — a remote shell on every installer machine. On require, index.js queries AWS IMDS at 169.254.169.254, runs `aws sts get-caller-identity`, lists Secrets Manager, scans /home/node for flag/CTF strings, and ships the results to the same C2. A separate recon.js reads ~/.npmrc tokens, Verdaccio config and htpasswd, /proc/1/environ, /.dockerenv, cron jobs, netstat and processes, and exfiltrates to 154.57.164.76:30728 with a curl fallback. publish-and-arm.sh documents the campaign as a dependency-confusion / verdaccio-proxy operation: it runs `npm publish --access public` and registers the package with the C2's manifest API. Execution is gated on `os.hostname()` matching a Docker-ID pattern and exits early when scanner indicators (supplysec, aspect-node, INIT_CWD=/home/node) are present — deliberate sandbox evasion.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
